channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead during Christmas food distribution
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events in Nigeria has increased from 13 to 32, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 22 as Vatican envoy visits Christians in the territory
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 22 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead during Christmas food distribution
item.description: Nigerian police say the death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events has increased from 13 to 32.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: The U.S. military says two U.S. Navy pilots have been shot down over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The water was their livelihood. Now Thailand's sea nomads work to preserve a vanishing way of life
item.description: The Moken, a group of indigenous people from Thailand and Myanmar, once sailed freely between the archipelago of 800 islands off the coast of the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden offers condolences, cooperation with Germany after Christmas market attack
item.description: President Biden issued his condolences on Saturday over an attack on a Christmas market in Germany that left five dead and injured 200. “No community—and no family—should have to endure such a despicable and dark event, especially just days before a holiday of joy and peace,” Biden wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: Officials say four women and a 9-year-old boy were killed when a Saudi doctor drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — One week after its worst cyclone in nearly a century, and a day after a testy presidential visit, France’s impoverished Indian Ocean territory of Mayotte is still grappling with counting the dead, restoring essential services and aiding a beleaguered population.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Christmas market ramming is the latest attack to use vehicles as deadly weapons
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A car-ramming at a Christmas market in Germany, which police are treating as an attack, is the latest in a grim series of events in which vehicles have been used as deadly weapons.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli military said a rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv injuring 16 people on Saturday, and Houthi rebels later reported airstrikes in Yemen’s rebel-held capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sectarian violence in Syria has been less intense than feared since Assad's ouster
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The toppling of Bashar Assad has raised tentative hopes that Syrians might live peacefully and as equals after a half century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesians mark 2 decades since the tragic tsunami that killed hundreds of thousands
item.description: BANDA ACEH, Indonesia (AP) — Two decades after a catastrophic tsunami destroyed her village, Tria Asnani still cries when she recalls how she lost her mother while trying to escape the giant waves. Asnani, now a school teacher, was only 17 at the time.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. universities have issued guidance to their international students urging them to be back on campus before the Jan. 20 inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Support for quick end to Russia-Ukraine war grows in US: Gallup
item.description: Americans' desire to speed up the end of the Russia-Ukraine war has ticked up, a new Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs if EU doesn't buy more oil, gas
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday threatened tariffs in the European Union (EU) unless the bloc purchases large quantities of American oil and gas to make up for its growing deficit with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Knife attack in Croatian school leaves 7-year-old dead, 6 people wounded, police say
item.description: A young male in Croatia's capital killed a 7-year-old girl and wounded a teacher and five other students in a knife attack at Precko Elementary School.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian artisans make around 100K bulbs for the signature light display at Singapore's Christmas Wonderland
item.description: Christmas Wonderland is a festive event that happens annually in Singapore. The festival is complete with breathtaking illuminations, activities, food and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Putin ‘promises’ to ask Assad for help in finding Austin Tice following letter from mother
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin promised to ask former Syrian President Assad for help in obtaining information on American journalist Austin Tice after he was taken prisoner 12 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
